meta.title: X2 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

hero.tagPill: BMW X2 • 2017–Present • 2 Generations (F39, U10) • B38, B48, B47, N47
hero.h1: X2 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW X2 engine across two generations — from the N47 diesel and B48 four-cylinder of the F39 to the B38 three-cylinder and electrified B48 of the U10. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for BMW's stylish crossover coupé. Backed by real UK X2 engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The X2 rule — style over substance: a B48 recon at £3,000–£5,000 against a £10,000–£20,000 F39 is 15–50% — ✅ safe on most examples. The U10 is too new for volume data, but the B-family engines are proven. The N47 diesel in early F39 cars carries the chain caveat — buy with paperwork.
★★★★☆ — the B38/B48/B47 are the most dependable; early N47 diesels carry the chain-tensioner caveat; the B48 in the M35i is proven and robust
B48B20 (F39/U10) and B47C20A/B — the modular B-family engines are the modern gold standard
Early N47D20C (pre-2018 F39) without documented timing-chain replacement
2019+ F39 sDrive 20i or xDrive 20d with B48/B47 — post-chain-revision, stylish UKL2 crossover, sensible running costs
F39 M35i xDrive with B48 — 306 hp, the most powerful X2 ever (before the U10 M35i)
N47 timing chain (early F39) / B48 cooling system — shared-platform data [BMW-VERIFIED]
🏆 The Style-Led X Family — the X1's sportier, coupé-styled sibling

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The X2 is BMW's style-led crossover — the X1's sportier sibling with a coupé roofline. The B-family engines define the modern cars; early N47 diesels carry the chain caveat. Buy the B48 petrol or B47 diesel in a 2019+ F39, or the U10 with confidence — and on any early N47, insist on chain paperwork.

H2: BMW X2 Generations — Find Your Vehicle

rows.metric: Sub-headline: Two generations, two platforms, one stylish crossover mission. The right X2 depends on which generation and which engine you choose.
BMW X2 F39 🔥 Most Enquired — B48/B47 drives volume [BMW-VERIFIED]
rows.value: 2017–2024 • B38, B48, N47, B47 — UKL2 platform (FWD-based)

Our Verdict: The original X2 — BMW's stylish crossover coupé based on the UKL2 platform shared with the X1 and Mini Countryman. The B48 petrol and B47 diesel are the dependable choices; early N47 diesels carry the chain-tensioner caveat. The M35i xDrive with 306 hp is the performance pick. Buy 2019+ for the revised B-family engines.
2024–present • B38, B48 (mild-hybrid), iX2 electric — FAAR platform

Our Verdict: The second-generation X2 — revealed October 2023, launched 2024. Available with petrol, mild-hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and fully electric iX2 powertrains. The B48 in the M35i xDrive produces 312 hp with 48V mild-hybrid assistance. The iX2 is BMW's first electric X2. Too new for volume reliability data, but the B-family record is impeccable.
The Generation Divide — The Line That Matters Most
Read the full comparison: BMW X2 F39 vs U10 →

rows.metric: The Badge Ambiguity — "20i" and "20d" Mean Different Things
rows.value: Correction applied (internal): The "20i" badge covers different engines depending on generation. On the F39 (2017–2024), it's the B48A20A/B (2.0L, 178–192 hp). On the U10 (2024+), it's also a B48 variant but with mild-hybrid assistance. The "20d" badge on the F39 covers the N47 (early cars) or B47 (2019+). The N47 carries the chain risk; the B47 does not. All share a badge. None share a reliability profile.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW X2 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW X2 — verified codes, specifications and real UK ownership data.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: B48A20 and B47C20A/B ratings: 90% confidence — these engines are well-documented across the BMW range (1/2/3 Series, X1, Mini). N47D20C rating: 85% confidence — the chain issue is extensively documented. B38A15 rating: Expert-knowledge tier — the three-cylinder is proven across Mini and the 1 Series. M35i B48 data: Pattern-data tier — documented by specialist consensus. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The F39 X2 (2017–2024) uses the UKL2 platform, sharing its engines with the X1 and Mini Countryman. The N47 diesel was used in early production (2017–2018); the B47 replaced it in 2019. Correction applied: The F39 X2 M35i xDrive uses a high-output version of the B48 engine producing 306 hp. This is the most powerful engine in the first-generation X2. Correction applied: The U10 X2 (2024+) uses the FAAR platform. The diesel option has been dropped in many markets. The iX2 is the first electric X2. Correction applied: The F39 X2 xDrive25e plug-in hybrid uses a B38A15 engine with an electric motor, producing 220 hp combined.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the X2 range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: Early F39 X2 diesel models equipped with the N47 engine may suffer from timing chain stretching or failure. Cold-start rattle is the warning; ignored, chain slip destroys the engine. Affects pre-2018 F39 20d models. £3,200–£5,500 recon [BMW-QUOTE].

The B48 engine's known weak points — water pump leaks, coolant expansion tank failures, and oil filter housing gasket oil leaks. Affects F39 and U10 20i/25i/M35i models. £400–£800 repair if caught early.
The engine mounts can fail prematurely, causing increased vibration and noise. Affects F39 B48 models. Check for vibration at idle on any used F39.
The B47 diesel's primary weak points — EGR cooler leaks and DPF clogging on urban-driven cars. Affects F39 20d models (2019+). Well-documented, repairable, far less severe than the N47's chain issue.
The B48's diverter valve can fail, causing power loss and engine light activation. Affects F39 and U10 B48 models. £400–£800 repair.
Not an engine failure — but a common non-engine X2 enquiry. The F39's xDrive transfer case can fail, causing driveline vibration and noise. Check on any high-mileage F39 xDrive.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW X2 Market Intelligence — What UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising — the F39 is a stylish used alternative to the X1 [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — new generation entering used market [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬇ Falling — the N47 is being phased out
⬆ Rising — more cars entering warranty-exit age

insights: Interesting insight: The X2 generates a different kind of enquiry than the X1 — buyers are often style-led, choosing the X2 for its coupé roofline over the X1's practicality. The engine questions are similar (N47 chain, B48 cooling), but the buyer profile is younger and more design-conscious. The M35i generates a disproportionate share of enquiries — performance buyers doing due diligence on the B48's limits. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

The F39 X2 is now entering affordable territory — £8,000–£15,000 for a used example. The B48 petrol cars are the ones to buy; the N47 diesel early cars need chain paperwork. A 2019+ F39 with the B47 diesel or B48 petrol is the sweet spot — the chain risk is gone, the styling is distinctive, and the values are sensible. (Editorial synthesis.)

The U10 X2 is too new for volume reliability data — but the B-family engines are proven across the BMW range. The iX2 is the future; the M35i with 312 hp and 48V mild-hybrid is the performance pick. Buy the U10 with confidence — the B48 is one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW X2 Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one X2 based on this page, buy a 2019+ F39 with the B48 petrol or B47 diesel — the B-family engines fixed the chain issue that defined the early N47 cars, and the F39's coupé styling is distinctive and desirable. If the budget only stretches to an early F39 N47, insist on documented timing-chain replacement — a £10,000 F39 without chain paperwork is the trap, not the bargain.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X2 Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a BMW X2 engine typically takes 8–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The X2 rule — style over substance: A £3,200–£5,500 N47 recon against a £8,000–£10,000 early F39 sits at 32–69% — ⚠️ watch. Against a £15,000–£20,000 F39, a B48/B47 recon at £2,800–£5,000 is 14–33% — ✅ safe. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Buy 2019+ F39 with B47/B48 or the U10 with B48 — the early N47 is the one to avoid without chain paperwork.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a BMW X2 engine typically takes 8–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. Against a £15,000–£20,000 F39, a B48/B47 recon at £2,800–£5,000 is 14–33% — ✅ safe. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Buy 2019+ F39 with B47/B48 or the U10 with B48 — the early N47 is the one to avoid without chain paperwork.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW X2 Engine Range Evolved — 2017 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW launched the X2 as the stylish crossover coupé sibling to the X1, on the UKL2 platform shared with the X1 and Mini Countryman.
eras.worthKnowing: Early F39 N47 diesels carry the chain-tensioner caveat. The B48 petrol cars are the dependable choice. The M35i xDrive with 306 hp is the performance pick.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The LCI replaced the N47 with the B47 diesel — the chain issue was finally resolved. The B48 petrol became the mainstream choice.
eras.worthKnowing: A 2019+ F39 with B47/B48 is the generation to buy — the chain risk is gone.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The second-generation X2 arrived on the FAAR platform — available with petrol, mild-hybrid, and fully electric iX2 powertrains. Diesel has been dropped in many markets.
eras.worthKnowing: The U10 is the future — B-family engines with 48V mild-hybrid assistance. The iX2 is the first electric X2. Too new for volume reliability data, but the B-family record is impeccable.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW X2?

profiles.verdict: The X2 is built for this — the coupé roofline and distinctive design make it the style-led alternative to the X1. The F39 with B48 petrol is the pick; the M35i adds performance without sacrificing style.
The X2 is a great commuter — the F39 18i with B38 or 20i with B48 returns good fuel economy, the UKL platform is comfortable, and the coupé styling makes it stand out. The U10 is even more refined.

A cheap early F39 N47 is not a cheap X2 — it's an expensive one with the first year's bills prepaid by nobody. A £12,000 F39 with B48 is the bargain; a £8,000 F39 N47 without chain paperwork is the trap.

The F39 M35i xDrive with 306 hp is a genuine hot crossover — the B48 is proven and responsive, the xDrive system delivers grip, and the styling is aggressive. The U10 M35i with 312 hp and 48V mild-hybrid is even quicker.
The X2 is BMW's stylish gateway crossover — approachable costs, distinctive design, and every mistake already documented. Start with a 2019+ F39 B48 or the U10 with confidence.

items.question: How much does a BMW X2 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned BMW X2 engine typically costs £2,800–£5,500 supply only [BMW-QUOTE] — from the B38 at £2,200–£3,800 to the N47 at £3,200–£5,500. The M35i B48 costs £3,800–£6,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

items.question: Which BMW X2 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The B48B20 petrol and B47C20A/B diesel in the 2019+ F39 and U10 — the B-family engines are the modern gold standard. The B38A15 three-cylinder is also proven and reliable.

items.question: Which BMW X2 engine should I avoid?
items.answer: No engine outright — but an early F39 N47D20C without a documented timing-chain replacement is the one purchase we consistently advise against. A 2019+ F39 with B47 or B48 has no such caveat. The B48 in the M35i is proven and robust.

items.question: How do I know if my X2 engine needs replacing?
items.answer: By engine: cold-start rattle (N47 timing chain), coolant loss (B48 cooling system), oil drips (B48 oil filter housing), EGR cooler leaks (B47 diesel), power loss and check engine light (B48 diverter valve). On early F39 N47, any rattling on startup is a warning sign. Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £2,800–£5,500 engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW X2 engine?
items.answer: Depends entirely on generation. Against a £15,000–£20,000 F39, a £3,000–£5,000 B48/B47 recon is 14–33% — ✅ safe. Against an £8,000 early F39, a £3,200–£5,500 N47 recon is 40–69% — ⚠️ watch. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Use our calculator for your car.

items.question: Which BMW X2 should I buy?
items.answer: 2019+ F39 with the B48 petrol or B47 diesel — the B-family engines fixed the chain issue, the UKL platform is practical, and the coupé styling is distinctive and desirable. For performance, the F39 M35i xDrive with 306 hp is the pick. For the future, the U10 with B48 or iX2 electric — buy it with confidence.

items.question: How do I find my BMW X2 engine code?
items.answer: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Or tell us your generation and variant (F39 18d, F39 20i, F39 M35i, U10 20i) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

items.question: BMW X2 vs X1 — which is more reliable?
items.answer: Mechanically they share engines across similar eras — the N47/B47/B48 story is identical. The X2 is essentially the X1 with a coupé roofline; reliability is the same. Buy on engine and year, not badge. The X1 offers more practicality; the X2 offers more style. Read the full head-to-head →
